metadata.description: Add mL1-ACE auxiliary calibration loss (training-time factor c=2) and integrate SWA/Multi-SWA into the factorial config structure. This PR creates the training-time infrastructure needed for the 24-condition debug run (4 models x 3 losses x 2 aux_calib).

references.ref: Barfoot et al. (2025). "Average Calibration Losses." IEEE TMI. arXiv:2506.03942
Izmailov et al. (2018). "Averaging Weights Leads to Wider Optima." UAI.

blocker.title: mL1-ACE reference implementation uses SegResNet, not our ModelAdapter
blocker.description: The cai4cai repo implements mL1-ACE with MONAI SegResNet. We need to adapt it to work with the ModelAdapter ABC (forward returns SegmentationOutput with logits).
blocker.fix: Extract only the loss computation (hard-binned L1-ACE), not the model code. The loss takes (logits, labels) tensors, which is model-agnostic.

phase.name: mL1-ACE Auxiliary Loss
phase.description:
Port the hard-binned L1-ACE (hL1-ACE) auxiliary calibration loss from the Average-Calibration-Losses repo. This is a TRAINING-TIME loss added alongside the segmentation loss, not a post-training method.
hL1-ACE = L1 distance between bin-level confidence and accuracy, using hard binning. It encourages the model to produce well-calibrated softmax outputs during training.
Formula: hL1-ACE = (1/B) * sum_{b=1}^{B} |acc(b) - conf(b)| where B=15 bins, acc(b) = mean(y_true) in bin b, conf(b) = mean(p_max) in bin b.

task.name: Implement hL1-ACE loss function
task.description:
Port the hard-binned L1-ACE calibration loss from cai4cai/Average-Calibration-Losses. This loss is differentiable through soft assignment (straight-through estimator for bin boundaries). Takes (logits, labels) and returns scalar loss.
Key design: The loss operates on the softmax probabilities, not raw logits. It bins predictions by confidence level and penalizes miscalibration per bin.
IMPORTANT: Use CC BY 4.0 attribution. Only port the loss computation, not the model or training loop code.

tdd_spec.tests: test_hl1ace_loss_returns_scalar test_hl1ace_loss_gradient_flows test_hl1ace_loss_perfect_calibration_near_zero test_hl1ace_loss_overconfident_positive test_hl1ace_loss_n_bins_configurable test_hl1ace_loss_batch_independence test_hl1ace_loss_binary_segmentation

task.acceptance_criteria: - Loss returns finite scalar for random inputs - Gradient flows through the loss (backward does not error) - Near-perfectly calibrated predictions produce near-zero loss - Overconfident predictions produce positive loss - Works with binary segmentation (C=2 channels) - Default n_bins=15 matches Barfoot et al. - CC BY 4.0 attribution in module docstring

task.name: Integrate hL1-ACE into compound loss factory
task.description:
Add a binary flag `with_aux_calib` to the loss factory that wraps any segmentation loss with hL1-ACE as an auxiliary term:
total_loss = seg_loss(logits, labels) + lambda_calib * hL1_ACE(logits, labels)
The lambda_calib weight defaults to 1.0 (equal weighting, as in Barfoot et al.). This creates the c=2 factor: {None, hL1-ACE} for each segmentation loss.
Register in the loss factory so Hydra config can enable it: loss_name: cbdice_cldice with_aux_calib: true aux_calib_weight: 1.0
task.acceptance_criteria: - with_aux_calib=False produces identical loss to baseline - with_aux_calib=True adds hL1-ACE term - aux_calib_weight scales the auxiliary term - All 3 segmentation losses work with aux_calib: dice_ce, cbdice_cldice, dice_ce_cldice - Loss factory creates correct compound loss from Hydra config

phase.name: SWA/Multi-SWA Factorial Config
phase.description: Integrate SWA and Multi-SWA as factorial levels (k=3: None, SWA, Multi-SWA) into the post-training config. The plugins already exist — this phase makes them configurable via Hydra for the factorial experiment.

task.name: Factorial post-training config schema
task.description:
Extend PostTrainingConfig to support factorial experiment specification. Add a `factorial_methods` field that lists which post-training methods to apply systematically (as opposed to the current plugin-by-plugin config).
The factorial design requires: - k=3 levels: [none, swa, multi_swa] - Each level produces a distinct checkpoint variant - Output naming: {run_id}_{method}.pt (e.g., abc123_swa.pt, abc123_multi_swa.pt) - The "none" variant is the original checkpoint (identity operation)
This is config-level work — the actual SWA/Multi-SWA execution already works.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Config accepts factorial_methods: [none, swa, multi_swa] - Invalid methods raise ValidationError - Checkpoint naming convention is deterministic - Config serializes to/from YAML

task.name: Factorial post-training execution loop
task.description:
Add a `run_factorial_post_training()` function that systematically applies each post-training method to each training checkpoint, producing the full k=3 checkpoint matrix.
Input: 72 training checkpoints (24 conditions x 3 folds) Output: 216 checkpoints (72 x 3 post-training methods)
The function iterates over (checkpoint, method) pairs and logs each variant to MLflow with tags: post_training_method={none|swa|multi_swa}.
task.acceptance_criteria: - "none" method returns checkpoint unchanged - SWA averages across folds of same condition - Multi-SWA produces n_models independent averages - Each variant is tagged in MLflow - Checkpoint file sizes are logged as metrics

phase.name: Training Config Extensions
phase.description: Add the with_aux_calib flag to the Hydra training config and create the factorial experiment base config.

task.name: Hydra config for aux_calib factor
task.description:
Add with_aux_calib and aux_calib_weight to the Hydra training config groups. Create configs/experiment/factorial_base.yaml that can be overridden:
python -m minivess.train \ experiment=factorial_base \ model=dynunet \ loss_name=cbdice_cldice \ with_aux_calib=true \ fold_id=0
This enables the full factorial sweep via Hydra CLI overrides.
task.acceptance_criteria: - with_aux_calib defaults to false (backward compatible) - Hydra override with_aux_calib=true works - factorial_base.yaml sets max_epochs=50, experiment tag - All 4 model configs work with factorial_base

task.name: Checkpoint size tracking
task.description:
Log checkpoint file sizes as MLflow metrics for the cost appendix. The factorial design produces 216 checkpoints — tracking sizes helps estimate storage costs and identify unexpectedly large checkpoints.
Log: checkpoint/size_mb, checkpoint/format (pth/onnx), checkpoint/n_params
task.acceptance_criteria: - Checkpoint size in MB logged to MLflow - Format (pth) tagged - Parameter count logged
